servinfo.title: Repair and Inspection Required after Accident

item.ptxt: All air bag system components and their mounting points, including electrical harness, must be inspected after an accident. If any component is damaged or deformed, it must be replaced even if air bag system has not activated.
Never use air bag system parts from another vehicle.
Do not attempt to repair parts below. If any defect is found for these parts, replace it.
Driver / Passenger air bag (inflator) modules
Knee air bag (inflator) module
Driver-side / passenger-side side-air bag (inflator) modules
Driver-side / passenger-side curtain air bag (inflator) modules
Driver / Passenger seat belt pretensioners
Driver-side / passenger-side forward impact sensors
Driver-side / passenger-side front side-impact sensors
Driver-side / passenger-side rear side-impact sensors
SDM
Contact coil
Air bag wire harnesses (in main harness, instrument panel harness and floor harness), passenger air bag harness and seat harness.
Proper operation of sensors and air bag system requires that any repairs to vehicle structure return it to its original production configuration.
Never use SDM which has detected a condition that meets any of deployment conditions.
Refer to Air Bag Diagnostic System Check when checking SDM.

servinfosub.title: Accident with Deployment / Activation – Component Replacement
servinfosub.ptxt: Replace the following components when driver and passenger air bags are deployed.

item.ptxt: Driver and passenger air bag (inflator) modules
Knee air bag (inflator) module
Driver-side / passenger-side seat belt pretensioners
SDM
Driver / Passenger forward impact sensors
Instrument panel

servinfosub.ptxt: Replace the following components when side-air bag and curtain air bag are deployed.

item.ptxt: Deployed side-air bag (inflator) module
Deployed curtain air bag (inflator) module
Driver-side / passenger-side front side-impact sensor
Driver-side / passenger-side rear side-impact sensor
SDM

servinfosub.title: Accident with or without Deployment / Activation – Component Inspections
servinfosub.ptxt: Inspect the following air bag system components after any crash, whether air bag system was activated or not. If any faulty condition is found in the following checks, replace faulty component.

item.ptxt: Steering column and steering lower shaft
Check for length, damage and bend according to Steering Column Inspection (Tilt Type) / Steering Column Inspection (Tilt and Telescopic Type) .
Steering column bracket
Check for damage and bend according to Steering Column Inspection (Tilt Type) / Steering Column Inspection (Tilt and Telescopic Type) .
Steering wheel and driver air bag (inflator) module
Check air bag (inflator) module for damage and installation condition.
Check trim cover (pad surface) for cracks.
Check wire harness and connector for damage and tightness.
Contact coil assembly
Check wire harness and connectors for damage and tightness.
Check contact coil case for damage.
Check contact coil circuit referring to Contact Coil and Its Circuit Check .
Instrument panel member and reinforcement
Check for any distortion, bending, cracking and other damage.
Check instrument panel for cracks and deformities.
Passenger air bag (inflator) module
Check for dents, cracks, damage and installation condition.
Check harness and connector for damage and tightness.
SDM
Check for external damage such as deformation, scratch, crack and peeled paint.
Check SDM for deformation which prevents its proper installation (gap between SDM and SDM plate, etc.).
Check connector or lead wire of SDM for scorch, melt or damage.
Check SDM connector and terminals for tightness.
Check DTC and follow referring corresponding diagnostic flow if any DTC is found.
Air bag wire harnesses and connectors
Check for damages, deformities and poor connections.
Refer to Intermittent Connection and Poor Contact Inspection .
Check wire harnesses for tightness.
Forward impact sensors
Check sensor (1) and apron side member (2) for damage, bend and rust.
Check connector (3) and lead wire (4) of forward impact sensor for scorch, melt and damage.
Front side-impact sensors
Check sensor (1) and side sill inner panel (2) for dents, cracks, deformation and rust.
Check sensor connector (sensor side and harness side) (3) or sensor lead wire (4) for damage, crack, scorch and melt.
Rear side-impact sensors
Check sensor (1) and quarter inner panel (2) for dents, cracks, deformation and rust.
Check sensor connector (sensor side and harness side) (3) or sensor lead wire (4) for damage, crack, scorch and melt.
Seat belt pretensioner
Check for dents, cracks, damage and installation condition.
Check harness and connector for damage and tightness.
Knee air bag (inflator) module
Check for dents, cracks, damage or looseness.
Check cover for cracks or deformation.
Check wire harness and connector for damage or tightness.
Seat belts and mounting points
Refer to Front Seat Belt Components .
“AIR BAG” warning light
Perform Air Bag Diagnostic System Check after vehicle is completely repaired.
Side-air bag (inflator) modules
Check for dents, cracks, damage or fitness.
Check seat back trim cracks or deformities.
Check wire harness and connector for damage or tightness.
Curtain air bag (inflator) modules
Check dents, cracks, damage or installation condition.
Check wire harness and connector for damage or tightness.
Check head lining for cracks or deformation.
